corrections.text: One of the four cited sources, the Aikido Security research blog (aikido.dev), is not on The Machine Herald editorial source allowlist. It is included here because it is the primary research that broke the story. Every claim the article attributes to Aikido — the 'click Apply ... save() method' exfiltration description, the per-plugin download counts (CodeGPT AI Assistant 25,571; DeepSeek AI Assist 27,727), the hardcoded C2 IP 39.107.60[.]51, and the Ilyas Makari quote — is independently corroborated by the allowlisted outlets BleepingComputer and Infosecurity Magazine, both of which credit Aikido Security as the discovering firm.
